mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) or negative mean returns during periods of declining FEDFUNDS over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals abrupt labor market deterioration, triggering recession fears that immediately suppress equity valuations and produce negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.4pp monthly threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue acceleration and sustains positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive MoM changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce a positive rank IC greater than 0.05 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side bottlenecks and rising input costs, which compress profit margins and trigger immediate short-term equity sell-offs.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods are followed by positive 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of observed cases.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL readings above 3.5% force expectations of restrictive monetary policy, which raises the equity risk premium and generates negative forward returns as investors de-risk portfolios.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in over 50% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5, with a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year rank IC for PAYEMS growth against 60-day forward returns falls below 0.02, or mean returns are negative during payroll expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between slowing real GDP growth and declining retail sales signals a sudden demand shock that triggers algorithmic risk-off positioning and immediate short-term equity drawdowns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no negative skew or positive mean when GDPC1 growth decelerates alongside falling RSAFS over a 4-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: An expansion in industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase signals successful capacity reallocation and rising corporate output, which generates positive equity returns as earnings expectations improve.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during recovery phases are followed by negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of cases, with a rank IC below 0.03.
